steps.title: Define what the word must mean in context
steps.content: Fabry says the human machine "is not effective" and "no longer answers the requirements of modern engineering." The phrase "answers the requirements" is parallel to "effective" — it describes whether something meets or satisfies a standard. The correct word must mean to satisfy or meet requirements.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the one that means 'satisfy/meet'
steps.content:
Substitute each choice into "no longer ______ the requirements of modern engineering":
  • Choice A — Explains: means to clarify or describe; requirements are not explained, they are met or not. ✗
  • Choice B — Rebuts: means to prove false; requirements cannot be disproven. ✗
  • Choice C — Defends: means to justify or argue for; the passage is about failing to meet requirements, not defending them. ✗
  • Choice D — Fulfills: means to satisfy or meet a demand — human workers can no longer fulfill what modern engineering demands. ✓
That is the correct answer.
